choices.text: Multi-stage build with pinned base image versions, BuildKit cache mounts, non-root user, multi-stage build, healthcheck, exec-form CMD. Combined with runtime flags: `--restart unless-stopped`, memory limits, read-only FS with tmpfs for writable paths, `--cap-drop=ALL`, log rotation, specific image tag. Each piece addresses one common mistake; together they form the modern production posture.

questions.explanation: Multi-stage + pinned + non-root + healthcheck + runtime hardening = production baseline.

questions.prompt: Quickest audit for security/bloat issues in an existing image?

choices.text: Three tools: `docker history image` shows layers, sizes, instructions — surfaces bloat from large layers. `dive image` interactive browser shows what each layer added; reveals leftover build artifacts, large unnecessary files, missing cleanup. `trivy image image` scans for known CVEs with severity. Run all three on an image and you'll surface most common issues within minutes. Beyond static scans, runtime checks: is it running as root? what user? `docker inspect` shows the configured user and runtime flags.

questions.explanation: history + dive + trivy = comprehensive image audit in minutes.
